design_ref: FROZEN_BIAS_LM_DESIGN.md ROUND-5 REVISION -- family B (anchor_directed) lambda mini-sweep added (fix 6, sec 11b-3), same grid discipline as family A's own lambda grid (ROUND-4 fix 2)

shared_dirs.verdict: PASS -- null case produces EXACTLY zero delta (bit-identical populations, as required by construction) in all seeds

anchor_directed.verdict: PASS -- null case produces EXACTLY zero delta (bit-identical populations, as required by construction) in all seeds

seed_noise_floor (cross-seed null-hypoth.note: Cross-seed null-hypothesis noise floor -- Arm2-analog and Arm1'-analog drawn from INDEPENDENT seeds, both at effect_strength=0.0 (no injected training effect). This, not the degenerate identical-input null cell, is the correct s_ref for the sensitivity bar: real Arm 2 vs Arm 1' measurements will always differ by at least this much seed/sampling noise even if training confers zero span_frac-relevant effect.

shared_dirs.verdict: PASS -- comparison detects an injected training-mediated effect at effect_strength>=0.3 (clears the mean_ref+/-2*s_ref bar), monotonically, while the null case (effect_strength=0.0) produces exactly zero delta (see check_null_is_exact).

anchor_directed.verdict: PASS -- comparison detects an injected training-mediated effect at effect_strength>=0.1 (clears the mean_ref+/-2*s_ref bar), monotonically, while the null case (effect_strength=0.0) produces exactly zero delta (see check_null_is_exact).

shared_dirs.note: Linear interpolation between the two grid points straddling the mean_ref+2*s_ref threshold -- this is the reproducible version of the round-3 attacker's own ~0.284 estimate (there, interpolated between the old grid's 0.2 and 0.3 points).
